There are 7 continents in the planet Earth.
The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.
The Earth orbits the Sun once every 365 days.
Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, known as H2O.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second.
Humans have 206 bones in the adult human body.
The Great Wall of China stretches thousands of miles across northern China.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth above sea level.
The Amazon River is the largest river in the world by water discharge volume.
The capital city of France is Paris.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight and carbon dioxide into food.
The human heart has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles.
The chemical symbol for gold is Au, derived from the Latin word aurum.
The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite and orbits the Earth.
Gravity is the force that attracts two bodies with mass toward each other.
The United States declared independence from Britain in the year 1776.
DNA carries the genetic instructions for the development and function of living organisms.
The average human body temperature is approximately 37 degrees Celsius or 98.6 degrees Fahrenheit.
The Sun is a star located at the center of our solar system and provides light and heat to Earth.
